mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and falling housing starts indicates a shift in consumer spending from durable goods to services, creating sector rotation that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production below zero confirms supply-side contraction and manufacturing weakness, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5d returns occur in >60% of negative MoM INDPRO months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 7.0% forces aggressive central bank tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns are positive in >65% of high inflation observations, or signal fails to predict direction in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction effect is negative or insignificant, or 20d returns are negative in >60% of joint signal occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0% confirms sustained economic expansion and strong aggregate demand, supporting higher corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows, or 60d returns are negative in >55% of expansionary quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling federal funds rates indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting manufacturing output, which reduces recession risk and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.
